title: The History of Animation
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Animation has (0) been entertaining audiences for over a century, evolving from simple hand-drawn images to sophisticated computer-generated imagery. The art form has captivated both children and adults, becoming a major part of global entertainment culture.||The earliest animations date back (9) to the late 19th century, when inventors created devices that showed sequences of drawings in rapid succession, creating the illusion of movement. Traditional animation involved drawing each frame (10) by hand on transparent sheets called cels. A feature-length animated film could require hundreds of thousands of individual drawings, making (11) it an extremely labour-intensive process.||Walt Disney revolutionised the industry with films like Snow White in 1937, (12) which was the first full-length animated feature. Disney pioneered many techniques, including the multiplane camera that created (13) a sense of depth in animated scenes. Japanese anime developed its own distinctive style and storytelling traditions, becoming hugely influential worldwide.||The introduction of computer animation in the 1990s transformed the industry completely. Pixar's Toy Story, released in 1995, was (14) the first entirely computer-animated feature film. Digital animation has (15) made it possible to create effects and visual styles that would have been impossible with traditional techniques. (16) Despite these technological advances, many animators argue that the fundamental principles of timing, movement, and storytelling remain the same regardless of the tools used.

example.text: Animation has (0) entertaining audiences for over a century.

questions.explanation: Expression 'date back to' means to originate from a certain period.
Expression 'by hand' indicates the manual method of drawing.
Object pronoun referring to the animation process.
Relative pronoun referring to the film Snow White.
Indefinite article: 'created a sense of depth'.
Definite article referring to the first example of a computer-animated film.
Present perfect: 'has made it possible' indicates a result up to now.
Preposition 'Despite' introduces contrast with technological advances.
